research_records.title: Short-course radiotherapy versus standard care in early-stage breast cancer: a randomized controlled trial
research_records.title_normalized: short course radiotherapy versus standard care in early stage breast cancer a randomized controlled trial
research_records.abstract: Background: Short-course radiotherapy may reduce treatment burden... Methods: 412 patients were randomized... Results: Non-inferior local control was observed... Conclusion: Short-course schedules are a viable option.
research_records.summary: A randomized trial of 412 early-stage breast cancer patients found that a shorter radiotherapy schedule was non-inferior to standard care for local tumor control, while reducing patient treatment time. Side-effect profiles were comparable between groups.
key_findings: Short-course radiotherapy was non-inferior for 3-year local control (94.1% vs 94.8%).
Grade 3+ skin toxicity was lower in the short-course arm (6% vs 11%).

extracted_tables.caption: Table 1. Baseline characteristics by treatment arm
